source's stun is conditional: only fires if target takes 3 hits during the 虎嗔 DoT buildup window (rare, multi-step chain). Current data grants a guaranteed full stun on every single activation of the tactic (rate 0.6 gates the whole tactic, but stun itself has no further gating) - a clear power overshoot vs a source that requires a rare conditional chain. No existing _note discloses this specific overshoot (existing _real focuses on the DoT/amp approximation only).

BUG: source says the reactive on-hit debuff (attacker's next damage -20%->40%) is gated by the SAME 45% chance that also gates the counter-hit (both fire together on one dice roll, per onHit dispatch: `if (rnd() >= t.rate) continue;` gates the whole block before hit()+applyEffects()). The current _approx note double-folds the 45% probability into the debuff value (45%x40%=18%), when the 45% was already consumed by the engine's onHit gate. Cross-checked against 剛烈不屈 (rate 0.4, when.on, stat add:38 raw undiscounted) and 魅惑 (rate 0.45, when.on, silence dur1 undiscounted) - both confirm the house convention: on-hit reactive effects use the FULL raw value, not EV-folded again. Remove the _approx/_note fields (no longer an approximation).
